message:
fix(fs): support non-UTF8 Unix paths in io-uring path_to_cstring helpers

Three separate path_to_cstring() functions in the fs module (dir.rs,
path_ops.rs, uring.rs) converted paths via Path::to_str() which
rejects valid non-UTF8 Unix paths — any filename containing bytes
like 0xFF that aren't valid UTF-8 would produce an InvalidInput error
even though the kernel accepts arbitrary byte sequences (except NUL).

Fix: Convert through OsStrExt::as_bytes() on Unix, which preserves
the raw byte representation. The existing NUL-byte rejection via
CString::new() is preserved since NUL is the only byte that cannot
appear in Unix paths.

Changes per file:
- src/fs/dir.rs: Replaced to_str().ok_or() with as_os_str().as_bytes()
  via `use std::os::unix::ffi::OsStrExt`. Added cfg-gated regression
  tests for non-UTF8 acceptance and NUL rejection.
- src/fs/path_ops.rs: Same path_to_cstring fix. Added matching
  regression tests with cfg gates.
- src/fs/uring.rs: Extracted inline path conversion in
  open_with_flags() to a shared path_to_cstring() function using the
  same OsStrExt approach. Moved the OsStrExt import to module scope.
  Added regression tests.

All tests are gated on #[cfg(all(target_os = "linux", feature = "io-uring", unix))]
or #[cfg(unix)] as appropriate for the platform-specific code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
